title: Canonical GJMS compositions
summary: Specializes abstract Juhl compositions to the canonical ambient GJMS family and verifies that the singleton composition is the ambient Laplacian power Δ̃ᴺ under the algebraic-identities interface.

mathematicalContent.heading: Canonical family
mathematicalContent.body: Given the ambient algebraic identities, each positive k is assigned the canonical abstract GJMS operator constructed from the kth ambient Laplacian power.

mathematicalContent.heading: Ordered composite
mathematicalContent.body: For I=(I₁,…,Iᵣ), the resulting unary operator is the ordered composition of the canonical family.

mathematicalContent.heading: Singleton theorem
mathematicalContent.body: Lean proves that the one-part composition (N) acts exactly by the Nth ambient Laplacian power.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the Juhl family obtained from the canonical abstract ambient GJMS operators.

correspondence.leanType: def canonicalGJMSComposition (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) {N : Nat} (I : Composition N) : Function Conf → Function Conf
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the ordered canonical GJMS composite indexed by a composition I.

correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalGJMSComposition_singleton (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) : canonicalGJMSComposition CalConf H (Composition.singleton N hN) = abstractToUnary CalConf (GJMS.canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H N)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Shows that the singleton composition is the underlying canonical order-2N GJMS operator.

correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalGJMSComposition_singleton_apply (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) (f : Function Conf) : canonicalGJMSComposition CalConf H (Composition.singleton N hN) f = (GJMS.canonicalAmbientGJMS CalConf H N).toWeightedTangentialOperator.toOperator f
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the singleton equality on an ambient function f.

correspondence.leanType: theorem canonicalGJMSComposition_singleton_apply_lapPow (H : CalConf.AlgebraicIdentities) (N : Nat) (hN : 0 < N) (f : Function Conf) : canonicalGJMSComposition CalConf H (Composition.singleton N hN) f = CalConf.lapPow N f
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Uses the canonical construction to identify the singleton composite with the Nth ambient Laplacian power.

references.note: Source for ordered GJMS compositions and coefficients.

references.locator: Direct ambient proof of Juhl's formulas
references.note: Ambient interpretation of the composition formula.

limitations: Only the singleton composition is simplified. No theorem here proves the full Juhl formula, maps weights through a nontrivial composition, or descends the composite to a concrete curved conformal operator; the canonical family itself depends on AlgebraicIdentities.
